title: Replication, Multi-Region Access, Backup, and Disaster Recovery · Go Build Room
summary: A delivery team incrementally builds RPO, RTO, replication, failover, and restore dossier, changes one file or failure, and keeps earlier HTTP, Go, accessibility, security, and resource-budget invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of RPO, RTO, replication, failover, and restore dossier while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate object durability, request availability, regional reachability, accidental deletion, corruption, account compromise, and application recovery.
Configure versioning, destination ownership, IAM, KMS, filters, delete-marker behavior, replication status, metrics, and failure remediation.
Evaluate Multi-Region Access Points, latency routing, active-active writes, consistency, replication lag, failover controls, and client endpoint support.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Durability Availability Recovery
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go file-delivery team handles case 4447 during a compliance restore drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate replication, independent backup, restore, and failover evidence; preserve accessible progress, cancellation, status, and recovery evidence; then change cache and retrieval cost assumptions and defend the new budget result. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Durability Availability Recovery.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate object durability, request availability, regional reachability, accidental deletion, corruption, account compromise, and application recovery. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Durability Availability Recovery, keep Durability Availability Recovery connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
